mentions.note: Caller Garrett owns ~300 shares; Cramer says hold on to it; new CEO, 4% yield, bouncing off lows for first time in long time; willing to bless owning it, maybe even buy another 100 if it hits $100; wants to have new CEO on show.
New CEO Mike Fideli taking over; lacks scale vs. Amazon/Walmart/Costco; must reinvent quickly like predecessor Cornell did with Canada exit.
Up 6.75% on strong quarter; new CEO catalyst; retail recovery play despite macro uncertainty.

New CEO Michael Fidelski delivering turnaround: margin expansion, accelerating Feb momentum, $2B investment plan. Guided FY26 net sales ~2% growth, 4.8% margin. Valuation 15x vs Walmart 44x, Costco 50x. Still a steal if execution holds.
Down on Day 2; expected retail strength not showing.
Had strong run pre-war, now hitting reversal as consumer caution sets in; big-box retailer exposed to discretionary spending weakness.
New management successfully bringing back old magic; expects repeat of terrific performance from three months ago; comparisons may be over.
32% earnings growth, 5.6% SSS (vs. 2.4% expected), raised full-year outlook; 15x earnings, 3.6% yield; turnaround story headed right direction; home-run quarter.
